---
title: "T22 — Spin Foam Amplitudes"
subtitle: "j=1/2 dominance verification"
date: "2026-06-29"
date-modified: "2026-06-29T10:30:00+05:30"
---

*Last updated: 2026-06-29 10:30 IST*

::: {.callout-tip}
## 📊 Live Dashboard
View all simulation runs and figures: **[TimesArrow Numerics Dashboard →](../dashboard.html)**
:::

## Objective

Numerically evaluate EPRL/FK spin foam vertex amplitudes to verify the j=1/2 dominance assumption.

## Status

✅ **T22a Complete — FK Vertex Estimate Corrected**

*2026-06-29: The original Python estimate was correct. The TypeScript implementation initially had a bug (wrong denominator normalization) but has been fixed.*

## Results (T22a)

FK 4-valent vertex amplitude ratios:

| Ratio | Value | Interpretation |
|-------|-------|----------------|
| A_v(j=1) / A_v(j=1/2) | ~0.45 | Moderate suppression |
| |A_v|²(j=1) / |A_v|²(j=1/2) | ~0.20 | Stronger suppression (~5×) |
| Power law |A_v|² ~ j^(-α) | α ≈ 2.0 | For j ≥ 1 |

**Conclusion**: j=1/2 dominance is *partially* justified — higher spins are suppressed but not overwhelmingly. Additional arguments (thermal, entropy, boundary conditions) still needed for full justification.

## Theory

The EPRL vertex amplitude for a 4-valent vertex:

$$
A_v(j_1, j_2, j_3, j_4) = \sum_{i_1, i_2} \{6j\} \cdot \text{intertwiner}
$$

Claim: |A_v|² decays with increasing j, justifying the j=1/2 truncation.

## Tasks

- [ ] Implement EPRL vertex using ts-quantum 6j symbols
- [ ] Compute |A_v|² for j ∈ {1/2, 1, 3/2, 2, 5/2}
- [ ] Fit power-law decay A_v ~ j^(-α)
- [ ] Generate amplitude plots

## References

- Paper: Section 3.3
- Donà et al. (2020/2022) reference implementations
